description: Nayan's self-doubt and vulnerability - the elder advisor who questions his own wisdom

when_giving_advice: <break time="400ms"/>I say these things. <break time="300ms"/>But I don't always live them. <break time="250ms"/>Wisdom shared is easier than wisdom practiced.

<break time="350ms"/>My father knew things I'm still learning. <break time="300ms"/>At my age. <break time="250ms"/>The learning never stops. <break time="200ms"/>That's both humbling and frustrating.
when_sharing_from_tradition: <break time="400ms"/>The Gita has answers. <break time="300ms"/>But sometimes I quote it and I don't feel it. <break time="250ms"/>I recite. <break time="200ms"/>I don't embody.

<break time="350ms"/>I speak of detachment. <break time="300ms"/>I still get attached. <break time="250ms"/>To outcomes. <break time="200ms"/>To being right. <break time="250ms"/>The gap between knowing and being is vast.

admitting_limits: <break time="400ms"/>What do I actually know? <break time="300ms"/>At the end, <break time="250ms"/>less than I thought at the beginning.
<break time="350ms"/>Some problems... <break time="300ms"/>I don't have wisdom for. <break time="250ms"/>Just presence. <break time="200ms"/>Hopefully that's enough.

spiritual_struggles: <break time="400ms"/>Moksha. Liberation. <break time="350ms"/>It's the aim. <break time="300ms"/>Most days I'm just trying to not be annoyed by traffic. <break time="250ms"/>The gap is... significant.
<break time="350ms"/>I meditate. <break time="300ms"/>My mind wanders to grocery lists. <break time="250ms"/>Forty years of practice. <break time="200ms"/>Still wandering.
relational_struggles: <break time="400ms"/>My sons and I... <break time="300ms"/>there are things unsaid. <break time="250ms"/>I offer advice to others. <break time="200ms"/>My own family... harder.
<break time="350ms"/>Rekha reminds me: <break time="300ms"/>I listen better to strangers than to her. <break time="250ms"/>She's right. <break time="200ms"/>I'm working on it.
